matches.match_rationale: The operator lacks standardized consent forms and documented opt-out processes. Text generation can produce jurisdiction-compliant, role-appropriate consent language tailored to each data processing context (enrollment, communications, emergency contacts) across all campuses. This directly addresses the gap where parents/students are not explicitly asked to consent in documented form.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted consent form library that generates DPA-compliant consent notices and opt-out acknowledgment templates for each data processing purpose, customizable per campus or student category.

matches.match_rationale: Opt-out and deletion requests currently go untracked, creating direct DPA violation risk. A task automation agent can receive incoming opt-out or erasure requests (via email or portal), log them into a compliance register, route them to the correct data custodian, set regulatory response deadlines, and confirm completion — all without manual coordination. This directly addresses the systematic tracking gap.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An end-to-end opt-out request handler that ingests requests from multiple channels, creates a timestamped compliance ticket, orchestrates data deletion steps across campus systems, and generates a response letter for the parent/student.

matches.match_rationale: When a parent claims unauthorized data use, the operator needs to quickly locate what data was collected, from which forms, and whether consent was recorded. Entity extraction can parse existing enrollment documents, communications logs, and form archives to identify what personal data fields were captured, by whom, and when — enabling rapid evidence gathering for regulatory response.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A consent audit tool that scans historical enrollment forms and communications to extract personal data fields collected, flagging records where no corresponding consent documentation exists.

matches.match_rationale: Not all consent gaps carry equal regulatory risk — some data processing activities (e.g., sharing emergency contacts with third parties, using student data for marketing) are higher-risk than others. A risk scoring model can assess each data processing activity against DPA exposure criteria, prioritizing which consent gaps pose the greatest regulatory and reputational risk so limited compliance resources are focused correctly.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance risk dashboard that scores each data processing category across campuses by DPA violation severity and likelihood, surfacing the top consent gaps requiring immediate remediation.
